title: dombeya buettneri
intro: Dombeya buettneri, also known as the "Forest Flower" or "Kakamega jasmine", is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dombeya buettneri include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
